subtopic: Draft Seeds Bill and Sand Mining
title: Draft Seeds Bill 2025 and India's Seed Sector
focus: Provisions of the Draft Seeds Bill 2025, comparison with the 1966 Act, classification of seeds, and government initiatives.
rationale: The seed sector is a highly examinable topic for UPSC, especially the classification of seeds (Breeder, Foundation, Certified), farmer rights under the new bill, and traditional landraces.
sectionExcerpt: Comparative Analysis of Seeds Act, 1966 and Draft Seeds Bill, 2025... Mandatory registration for every seed variety before trade... Farmers can save, exchange, and sell their seeds... Overview of India's Seed Sector: 5th largest Seed Industry... Recognizes three generations of seeds: Breeder Seed, Foundation Seed, Certified Seed... Key Initiatives: Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers' Rights Act, 2001... Seed Village Programme... Landraces are genetically diverse crop populations...
evidenceBullets: Draft Seeds Bill 2025 mandates registration for every seed variety before trade and incorporates biotech norms.
Farmers can save, use, exchange, or sell their seeds without brand registration, but cannot sell under a brand name.
Breeder Seed is the progeny of the nucleus seed; Foundation Seed is the progeny of breeder seed; Certified Seed is the progeny of foundation seed.
Odisha launched a roadmap to integrate Landraces (genetically diverse crop populations evolved with traditional practices) into formal seed systems.
Seed Village Programme (Beej Gram Yojana) aims to improve farmers' saved seed quality.

subtopic: Draft Seeds Bill and Sand Mining
title: Sand Mining and Environmental Regulations
focus: Supreme Court rulings, environmental impacts, and the legal framework governing sand mining in India.
rationale: Sand mining regulations, especially the mandatory nature of replenishment studies and environmental clearances (Deepak Kumar case), are critical for environment and ecology questions.
sectionExcerpt: Supreme Court (SC) observed that replenishment data is a mandatory prerequisite for environmental clearance in addition to District Survey Report (DSR)... Deepak Kumar vs State of Haryana (2012): SC made environmental clearance mandatory for all minor mineral extractions, including sand... EIA Notification (2016 Amendment): Cluster-based assessments and making replenishment studies a key part of DSR.
evidenceBullets: Supreme Court ruled that a replenishment study is a mandatory prerequisite for environmental clearance for sand mining.
Deepak Kumar vs State of Haryana (2012) made environmental clearance mandatory for all minor mineral extractions, including sand.
EIA Notification (2016 Amendment) introduced cluster-based assessments and made replenishment studies a key part of the District Survey Report (DSR).
Unrestricted sand mining causes riverbank erosion, lowers groundwater tables, and increases flood risks.

subtopic: Draft Seeds Bill and Sand Mining
title: Miscellaneous Initiatives: BRICS, UN-India, and RECLAIM
focus: Recent international and national frameworks related to land restoration, capacity building, and mine closure.
rationale: Short, factual news items that often appear as match-the-following or statement-based questions in Prelims.
sectionExcerpt: BRICS Land Restoration Partnership: Launched at 15th BRICS Agriculture Meet in Brazil to address land degradation... UN-India Global Capacity-Building Initiative: Share India's development experiences with Global South nations... Implemented through new UN India SDG Country Fund and ITEC... RECLAIM Framework: Coal Controller Organisation under Coal Ministry and Heartfulness Institute launched it for inclusive community engagement throughout mine closure.
evidenceBullets: BRICS Land Restoration Partnership was launched at the 15th BRICS Agriculture Meet in Brazil to address land degradation and desertification.
UN-India Global Capacity-Building Initiative shares India's development experiences with the Global South, implemented via the UN India SDG Country Fund and ITEC.
RECLAIM Framework was launched by the Coal Controller Organisation and Heartfulness Institute to guide community engagement during mine closure and post-closure phases.
